title: [BUG] Outdated dependencies causing deprecation warnings in Jenkins build (Node.js + ExcelJS)
author: Nikmakwana94
body:
## ЁЯРЫ Bug Report

Lib version: 4.4.0

I am encountering several deprecation warnings in my Jenkins build due to outdated dependencies. Upon investigating, it appears that the exceljs package is the root cause, as it uses older versions of transitive dependencies like rimraf, glob, fstream, and inflight. Below are the warnings from my Jenkins build logs:
```  
npm WARN deprecated rimraf@2.7.1: Rimraf versions prior to v4 are no longer supported
npm WARN deprecated fstream@1.0.12: This package is no longer supported.
npm WARN deprecated inflight@1.0.6: This module is not supported, and leaks memory. 
npm WARN deprecated glob@7.1.6: Glob versions prior to v9 are no longer supported 
```

## Environment

- Node.js version: 20
- Express.js version: 4.21.2
- Mongoose version: 7.8.6
- ExcelJS version: 4.4.0

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install exceljs version 4.4.0 in a Node.js + Express project.
2. Run the following commands:
    
```
npm ls rimraf
npm ls glob
npm ls fstream
npm ls inflight
```
3. Observe that exceljs is listed as the parent package for these outdated dependencies.
4. Run the Jenkins build, and note the warnings in the logs.

**Expected Behavior**
The exceljs package should update its transitive dependencies to avoid deprecation warnings and ensure compatibility with modern versions of Node.js.

**Actual Behavior**
The build produces deprecation warnings due to outdated dependencies used by exceljs.

**Possible Solution**

- Update the dependencies (rimraf, glob, fstream, and inflight) to their latest versions.
- Replace any deprecated dependencies with actively maintained alternatives if applicable.

**Questions**
1. Are there plans to update the transitive dependencies in exceljs to resolve these warnings?
2. If not, would you recommend a workaround or patch to mitigate this issue until an update is released?

## Additional Information
Below is the full dependency tree for exceljs showing the outdated dependencies:


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ef144d85-2b36-4166-9912-5bf8d1703989)

comments.author: dkindt
comments.body: @Siemienik Any chance we can get an update on this?

comments.author: StephanKuehn
comments.body:
We are currently using **exceljs 4.4.0** in an Angular 20 application (Node 20 environment) and are seeing multiple deprecation warnings during a fresh `npm install`.

Relevant dependency chain from our project:

```
exceljs@4.4.0
 тФЬтФА archiver@5.3.2
 тФВ   тФФтФА archiver-utils тЖТ glob@7.2.3 тЖТ inflight@1.0.6
 тФЬтФА fast-csv@4.3.6
 тФВ   тФФтФА lodash.isequal@4.5.0
 тФФтФА unzipper@0.10.14
     тФФтФА fstream@1.0.12
         тФФтФА rimraf@2.7.1 тЖТ glob@7.2.3
```

This results in the following npm warnings:

* inflight@1.0.6 (memory leak, deprecated)
* [[glob@7.x](mailto:glob@7.x)](mailto:glob@7.x) (deprecated, security notices in older versions)
* [[rimraf@2.x](mailto:rimraf@2.x)](mailto:rimraf@2.x) / 3.x (unsupported)
* fstream@1.0.12 (no longer maintained)
* lodash.isequal@4.5.0 (deprecated)

In our case, **exceljs is used at runtime (browser-side export functionality)**, so these transitive dependencies appear in the production dependency tree. While they are not currently flagged as high/critical vulnerabilities, they do trigger internal CI warnings and security scans.

Would it be possible to:

* Upgrade `unzipper` to a version that no longer depends on `fstream`
* Update the `archiver` / `glob` chain to remove `glob@7` and `inflight`
* Replace `lodash.isequal` where feasible

We are running modern Node (22.21.x) and would be fully compatible with dependency updates that drop legacy Node support.

Thanks for your work on ExcelJS тАФ we just wanted to provide additional real-world impact data from a current Angular setup.
